headline: OpenAI confirmation cycle. Musk v. Altman trial is now in week 2 with Greg Brockman testifying May 5 and Sam Altman expected May 11. New in-window disclosure: Musk's pre-trial settlement texts to Brockman, including alleged threat 'By the end of this week, you and Sam will be the most hated men in America' — entered as evidence by OpenAI's lawyers. Liability phase concludes May 21 with advisory jury verdict. Per methodology v1.2 verdict-pending rule, trial-disclosed evidence is not yet scored as final until the verdict. Scores held at published levels. Sub-threshold delta of 0; band Developing sustained; mandatory reassessment May 21.
key_evidence: Trial week 2 underway: Greg Brockman testimony began May 5; Sam Altman expected to testify week of May 11; Satya Nadella may testify as Microsoft co-defendant.
Musk pre-trial settlement texts to Brockman two days before trial began: settlement offer followed by alleged threat. OpenAI lawyers entered the texts as evidence.
Liability phase concludes May 21 with advisory jury verdict — mandatory reassessment trigger.
Trial scope: breach of charitable trust, nonprofit-to-PBC governance conversion, Microsoft co-defendant theory ($150B at stake).
Pentagon classified deal participation continues — already scored.
No new safety-specific or welfare-related evidence beyond ongoing trial disclosures.

evidence.finding: Musk reached out to Brockman seeking settlement two days before trial; OpenAI lawyers entered alleged threat texts as evidence in trial week 2.
Trial enters week 2 with focus shifting to Brockman; Altman expected week 3.
Trial week 2 confirmed; nonprofit-to-PBC conversion, charitable trust, and Microsoft co-defendant scope continued.

scoring_rationale.ACC: 1.8 unchanged. Trial proceedings are external accountability mechanism in process; no entity-side acknowledgment shift in window.
scoring_rationale.INT: 1.7 unchanged. Trial disclosures are evidence-in-litigation, not yet adjudicated. Per methodology v1.2 verdict-pending rule, settlement texts and trial-conduct evidence will be scored when the May 21 advisory verdict provides external adjudicative finding.

negativeSignals: Trial week 2 disclosures continuing — Musk settlement attempt followed by alleged threat. Pentagon classified deal participation continues.
positiveSignals: OpenAI's litigation conduct (entering Musk's threat texts as evidence) is consistent with discoverability transparency, not active suppression. Trial process itself is functioning as external accountability mechanism.
sector_context: OpenAI is one of two AI Labs entities with active major litigation in window (alongside Anthropic's DC Circuit case). Methodology applies the same verdict-pending posture to both.
open_actions_resolved: Trial week 2 disclosures captured as evidence-in-litigation (not yet scored)
open_actions_carried: May 21 advisory verdict: mandatory full reassessment trigger
Sam Altman week-3 testimony: capture in next cycle
Satya Nadella testimony if delivered: cross-reference with Microsoft assessment
Pentagon 'full discretion' carve-out for safety mechanisms remains unresolved
